digest.top_line: OLIGOMA: mPFS 35.8 vs 20.4mo (HR 0.48) — first breast-specific RCT PFS signal for MDT in oligoMBC.
digest.tldr: Breast dominated today: locoregional RT questions resolved (IMPORT HIGH SIB non-inferior at 10yr, EORTC IM-MS OS null at 20yr) while OLIGOMA opens oligoMBC MDT as a phase III target. OligoCare's 2447-pt registry flags CRC as the outlier with 19.6% 3-yr local failure despite highest doses.

sites.intro: Five breast trials spanning RT technique, fractionation, IM-MS, RT omission, and oligometastatic MDT — a broad locoregional portfolio day.

studies.name: IMPORT HIGH (ISRCTN47437448)
studies.tldr: IMPORT HIGH 10yr: SIB 48Gy/15F IBTR 3.7% vs sequential 40+16Gy 3.5% -- non-inferior, low late toxicity.
details: 🔍 Phase III RCT, N=2617, 76 UK hospitals (2009-2015); 1:1:1; pT1-3 pN0-N3a M0 requiring boost after BCS
🔗 5-yr data (Lancet 2023): IBTR 1.9%, 2.0%, 3.2% for same three arms -- 10-yr rates roughly double but tracking same rank order
📊 OS difference vs 40Gy at 10yr: -0.5% (-3.0, 2.8) for 48Gy; +1.5% (-1.4, 5.1) for 53Gy -- no significant OS difference
📊 Late mod/marked AEs at 10yr: breast distortion/shrinkage <18%, induration <10%, telangiectasia <2%, breast oedema <2% across all arms
⚠️ 53Gy arm shows numerically higher IBTR (5.5% vs 3.5-3.7%) without OS benefit -- further dose escalation not supported
❓ Whether SIB benefit persists in pts receiving contemporary systemic therapy (CDK4/6i, neoadjuvant pembrolizumab) remains unaddressed

studies.name: APBI-IMRT Florence (NCT02104895)
studies.tldr: APBI-IMRT Florence 15yr: IBTR 7.7% vs 4.2% (HR 1.57, p=0.17) — no difference in locoregional control, DM, or OS.
details: 📊 15-yr IBTR: APBI 7.7% vs WBI 4.2%, HR 1.57 (95% CI 0.82-3.04), p=0.17 — not statistically significant
🔍 excess IBTR in APBI arm driven by new ipsilateral primaries (5.9% vs 2.7%, p=0.09), not true local relapse (2.1% vs 1.6%, p=0.75)

details.text: 📊 Key outcomes at 15 years (ITT), APBI vs WBI

details: 🔍 phase III RCT, N=520, 1:1 APBI (30Gy/5#) vs WBI (50Gy/25# + 10Gy boost); selected early BC: BCS, pT <25mm, FSM ≥5mm, age >40; accrual 2005-2013
🔗 consistent with IMPORT LOW and GEC-ESTRO APBI trials showing non-inferiority in selected low-risk pts at 5-10yr; 15yr data now longest mature APBI-IMRT f/u
⚠️ numerically higher IBTR rate (7.7% vs 4.2%) remains; trial powered for equivalence margin Δ5% at 5yr — 15yr absolute gap of 3.5% approaches that margin even if p=0.14
❓ whether new ipsilateral primaries reflect true RT field-miss or second primary biology — implications for patient selection and surveillance remain open

studies.name: OLIGOMA (NCT04495309)
studies.tldr: OLIGOMA: mPFS 35.8 vs 20.4 mo (HR 0.48, p=0.021) favoring MDT + systemic over systemic alone in oligometastatic breast cancer.
details: 📊 Co-primary QoL at 12 wks: EORTC QLQ-C30 72.2 vs 74.3; delta -2.1 (95% CI -9.2 to 5.1) -- non-inferiority margin -10 pts met
🔍 Phase II RCT, N=87 (43 MDT arm, 44 control); oligoMBC any line, max 5 lesions; majority ER/PR+ HER2- in 1L; >80% had 1-3 mets, 2/3 lesions bone
⚠️ Recruitment stopped early (<20% of initial planned N, <40% of amended target); wide CI on HR (0.25-0.91) reflects severe underpowering -- positive p-value must be interpreted with caution
🔗 vs SABR-COMET (Lancet 2019): cross-histology MDT signal included breast; OLIGOMA is first breast-specific RCT with a PFS benefit, not just OS signal in mixed cohort
❓ OS immature; multiple larger ongoing trials (TAORMINA, STEREO-SEIN, LARA, OLIGAMI) needed to define patient selection and confirm OS benefit

studies.name: EORTC IM-MS (22922/10925)
studies.tldr: EORTC IM-MS 20yr: OS identical (61.0% vs 61.8%, HR 1.00) with IM-MS RT in stage I-III breast cancer.
details: 📊 1° EP OS at 20yr (ITT): 61.0% (+IM-MS) vs 61.8% (-IM-MS), HR=1.00 (95% CI 0.90-1.10), p=0.967 — null result
📊 BCM at 20yr: 18.6% vs 22.4%, HR=0.82 (0.72-0.95), p=0.006 — significant BCM reduction persists
📊 Non-BCM mortality at 20yr: 20.4% vs 15.8%, HR=1.26 (1.09-1.46), p=0.002 — competing-cause deaths higher in irradiated arm, offsetting BCM benefit
📊 pN0 subgroup at 20yr: DFS 53.9% vs 53.6% HR=0.93 (0.81-1.07), p=0.318; DMFS 67.2% vs 67.4% HR=0.93 (0.78-1.10), p=0.397 — no signal in node-negative pts

🔗 DBCG IMN2 (Lancet Reg Health Eur 2024): mean heart doses 4-9x lower than EORTC IM-MS; modern IM-MS RT reduced distant metastasis, BCM, and improved OS in node-positive pts at 15yr — suggests cardiac toxicity from older technique explains non-BCM excess mortality here
⚠️ Higher non-BCM death rate in irradiated arm likely reflects excess cardiac toxicity from 1990s-era technique (MHD ~3-8x higher than modern IM-RT); limits OS interpretation for current practice
❓ Whether modern low-dose IM-MS RT (MHD 1.2-2.3 Gy per DBCG IMN2) can achieve BCM benefit without the non-BCM mortality penalty remains the key question for node-positive pts

studies.name: DBCG RT Natural
studies.tldr: DBCG RT Natural: PBI omission yielded 9.8% vs 1.5% 5-yr LR at 4yr median f/u in low-risk breast conservation pts.
details: 📊 1° EP (5-yr invasive LR): 9.8% (no PBI arm, CIF 5.9-14.9%) vs 1.5% (PBI arm) at median 4yr f/u; 39 of 41 LR events in no-PBI arm
📊 LR by PBI + ET status (4yr CIF) — comparison values omitted (cell number "1.3" not verified in source)
🔍 Phase III RCT; N=926 planned; eligibility: ≥60yr, pT1N0, breast conservation, HER2-normal, grade 1-2, limited DCIS, margin ≥2mm; PBI arm 40Gy/15fr; trial stopped early per interim stopping rules
🔗 Validates PRIME II / CALGB 9343 omission signal but with harder LR endpoint -- omission group here hitting ~10% at 4yr, above the pre-specified 4% acceptable threshold
⚠️ No-PBI arm was partially self-selecting (pts opting out of RT), introducing selection bias; ET adherence suboptimal in -ET/-RT group driving the 12.2% tail
❓ Whether -RT +ET (3.7% CIF) is acceptable in shared decision-making for truly low-risk pts who decline RT remains open -- curves still separating at 4yr

open_questions: Does modern low-dose IM-MS RT (MHD ~1.2-2.3 Gy per DBCG IMN2) recover the BCM benefit seen in EORTC 22922 without the non-BCM mortality penalty from 1990s-era technique?
Is -RT +ET (3.7% 4-yr LR, DBCG RT Natural) acceptable in shared decision-making for truly low-risk elderly pts who decline RT, given curves still separating?
Can OLIGOMA's PFS signal (HR 0.48, but n=87, stopped early) be confirmed by TAORMINA/STEREO-SEIN/LARA with OS endpoints and pre-specified subgroups?

studies.name: RCC SBRT
studies.tldr: RCC SBRT: 💯% local control at 5 years reported; no effect size beyond LC reported in source tweets.
details: 📊 Local control: 100% at 5 years per source tweet; no other effect sizes reported in source tweets
🔍 Disease: renal cell carcinoma treated with SBRT; trial design, N, arms, and endpoints not specified in source tweets
⚠️ No phase, randomization, or comparator arm described — likely single-arm or registry series; 100% LC figures in small SBRT series are common and may reflect selection bias
🔗 Consistent with prior SBRT-RCC series (e.g., IROCK consortium data): 5-yr LC ~90-97% across histologies, though patient selection varies widely
❓ OS, CSS, distant control, and toxicity data not reported in source tweets; follow-up duration and N unknown

open_questions: What are the OS, CSS, and toxicity outcomes for RCC SBRT beyond local control, and what patient selection criteria drove the reported 100% LC?

studies.name: OligoCare (EORTC)
studies.tldr: OligoCare registry: SBRT for oligomets achieves 88.6% local control at 3 yrs across 2447 pts, 57 institutions.
details: 📊 Local in-field progression: 5.0% at 1 yr, 11.4% at 3 yrs (local control 88.6% at 3 yrs); median f/u 31 months
🔍 Prospective registry, N=2447 pts / 3533 lesions, July 2019-July 2025, 57 institutions; de novo OMD outperformed repeat OMD (higher delivered dose)

details.text: 📊 Local in-field progression at 1 and 3 yrs by primary tumor type

details: ⚠️ CRC had highest local failure at 3 yrs (19.6%) despite receiving highest median dose per fraction — highlights relative radioresistance; authors flag need for combination tx or dose escalation
🔍 Minimum PTV dose identified as most critical technical factor for local control; dose optimization emphasized in conclusions
🔗 Real-world registry data complement randomized evidence (SABR-COMET, STOMP, ORIOLE) showing OS/PFS benefit of SBRT for oligomets; this is the largest prospective OMD-SBRT dataset to date
❓ No OS or PFS data reported in this interim; systemic therapy heterogeneity across 57 institutions limits dose-response interpretation
studies.key_figure_caption: Local in-field progression by primary tumor type. 1-yr: CRC 4.1%, Breast 9.3%, NSCLC 6.0%, Prostate 2.7%. 3-yr: CRC 19.6%, Breast 11.3%, NSCLC 9.8%, Prostate 8.1%.

open_questions: Why does CRC have disproportionately high 3-yr local failure (19.6%) despite receiving the highest median dose per fraction in OligoCare -- radiobiology, target delineation, or fractionation schedule?
Will OS/PFS data from OligoCare's mature follow-up replicate the survival signals seen in SABR-COMET and STOMP in a real-world registry population?

studies.name: PRIME Trial (NCT03561961)
studies.tldr: PRIME interim: 5-fx SBRT non-inferior to 25-fx modHypo with pelvic RT + ADT; Grade 3+ toxicity <1% both arms; mature efficacy pending.
details: 🔍 Phase III non-inferiority RCT; N~434; high-risk/node-positive non-metastatic PCa; both arms: whole-pelvis RT + 2yr ADT; accrual 2018-2023 (Tata Memorial, India)
💊 Arm A: 36.25 Gy/5 fx (7.25 Gy/fx) every other day; Arm B: ~68 Gy/25 fx (2.7 Gy/fx); pelvic nodal 25 Gy/5 fx both arms
📊 1° EP BFFS (Phoenix): no inferiority signal at interim (1-2yr f/u); no effect size reported in source tweets -- mature 4-5yr data awaited
🔗 vs HYPO-RT-PC (10-yr): FFS 72% vs 65%, adj HR 0.84 (95% CI 0.69-1.03) non-inferior; but node-negative, no ADT, no pelvic RT -- PRIME tests a meaningfully higher-risk population
⚠️ 1-2yr f/u insufficient to adjudicate non-inferiority for PCa BFFS; no HR or CI reportable from interim -- practice change requires mature 4-5yr data
❓ PEACE-2 (3yr ADT, PSMA staging) showed no WPRT benefit (bPFS HR 0.97); POP-RT (2yr ADT) did (HR 0.50) -- PRIME's pelvic RT gain uncertain at maturity

open_questions: Will PRIME's 5-fx SBRT non-inferiority hold at 4-5yr BFFS maturity in high-risk/node-positive PCa with pelvic RT + 2yr ADT?
Does pelvic nodal RT add benefit in this population given conflicting signals from PEACE-2 (no benefit) vs POP-RT (HR 0.50)?

Ten-year results of the IMPORT HIGH trial
ISRCTN47437448: Dose escalated simultaneous
integrated boost radiotherapy in early breast cancer
Background & Methods
• IMPORT HIGH: tested simultaneous
integrated boost (SIB) escalation to:
• Regions at highest risk of recurrence
with a modest dose reduction to whole
breast distant to tumour at lower risk of
recurrence
• All delivered in 3 weeks
5-year IBTR rates (95%CI):
40Gy/15F + 16Gy/8F: 1.9% (1.2, 3.1)
48Gy/15F (3.2Gy/F): 2.0% (1.2, 3.2)
53Gy/15F (3.5Gy/F): 3.2% (2.2, 4.7)
48Gy/15F non-inferior in absolute terms with similar
or reduced side effects
Do these results hold-up at 10 years?
Coles et al. Lancet 2023; 401: 2124-37

conserving surgery for invasive early
breast cancer (pT1-3, pNO-pN3a, MO)
requiring tumour bed boost RT

Conclusion & Take-Home Message
15-19 MAY 2026
STOCKHOLM, SWEDEN
'ESTRO
2026
0000g
• At 10 years, IBTR event incidence is low in this higher-than-average risk breast cancer
group, whether boost is delivered sequentially or simultaneously
• Incidence in both 40+16Gy and 48Gy groups at 10 years remains lower than the 5%
estimate for control group at 5 years in original sample size calculations
• SIB is a safe treatment with reduced patient visits
• Further escalation of boost dose does not appear advantageous
• These results reaffirm use of 48Gy SIB in this population as standard of care

ESTRO

Discussion
• OLIGOMA is the first RCT to demonstrate a PFS-benefit from MDT in
patients with oligometastatic breast cancer.
• Majority had ER/PR pos. HER2 neg. disease in the first line-setting
• >80% of patients had 1-3 metastases, 2/3 of lesions were bone metastases.
• Short-term quality of life was not impaired by MDT.
• Recruitment was stopped early (< 20% of initial, < 40% of amended #)
• Numerous trials are still ongoing (incl. TAORMINA, STEREO-SEIN, LARA,
CLEAR, COSMO, ARCHER, ISTMET, OLIGAMI).
• Comprehensive analyses are needed to determine which patients benefit
most.

Conclusion & Take-Home Message
"ESTRO
2026
At 15 years median of follow up, the phase III APBI-IMRT Florence trial (NCT02104895)
demonstrates durable oncological outcomes with PB in selected patients with early breast cancer
Although a numerical increase in IBTR was observed, this was largely attributable to new
primary tumours rather than true local recurrences.
No differences were observed in loco-regional control, distant metastasis, breast cancer-
specific survival or overall survival.
These findings provide mature evidence supporting PBl as a safe de-escalation strategy and
reinforce its role as a standard treatment option in appropriately selected patients

PRIME Trial (NCT03561961): A Phase III Randomized Trial of
Extreme Hypofractionated (5-Fraction SBRT) vs Moderate
Hypofractionated Radiotherapy With Pelvic Nodal Irradiation
and ADT in High-Risk and/or Node-Positive Prostate Cancer
ESTRO
2026
STOCKHOLM
Source / Abstract No.:
ESTRO 2026 - Presented Abstract
(Tata Memorial Centre, Mumbai, India)
Abstract No. LBAxx
Presentation Author:
Dr. Indranil Mallick
Tata Memorial Centre,
Mumbai, India
Trial Name / Acronym:
PRIME
(Prostate Radiotherapy: Investigating
Moderate and Extreme Hypofractionation)
1. TRIAL OVERVIEW
2. TRIAL DESIGN
• Phase III, open-label, randomized, non-inferiority
trial conducted by Tata Memorial Centre &
collaborating centers in India.
Arm A
Extreme Hypofractionation
• Objective: To determine whether ultra-short-course
SBRT (5 fractions) is non-inferior to moderate
hypofractionation (25 fractions) in terms of
biochemical failure-free survival (BFFS) in
high-risk, very high-risk and/or node-positive
non-metastatic prostate cancer.
• 36.25 Gy in 5 fractions
(7.25 Gy per fraction)
• Every other day
• Accrual Period: 2018 - 2023
• Accrual Completed: Yes (-434 patients)
• Median Follow-up: Interim early data up to 1-2 years
(mature 4-5 year efficacy pending)
Eligibility Criteria
• High-risk, very high-risk
and/or node-positive
non-metastatic
prostate cancer
• ECOG 0-2
• Life expectancy: 10 years
• PSMA PET/CT staging
allowed

6. QUALITY OF LIFE (INTERIM FINDINGS)
Assessed using:
• EORTC QLQ-C30 (Core)

/ Urinary and bowel domains remained stable and comparable between arms.
/ No clinically meaningful deterioration in global health status/Qol.
/ Transient declines during/soon after treatment with recovery over time.
/ADT-related sexual/hormonal effects were similar between arms.
Key Point
5-fraction SBRT provides similar
7. CONCLUSION
The PRIME trial is the first randomized phase Ill study evaluating 5-fraction SBRT with whole-pelvis RT and ADT in high-risk and/or
node-positive prostate cancer. Interim results from ESTRO 2026 demonstrate excellent safety, low toxicity, and comparable early
efficacy and QoL between extreme hypofractionation and moderate hypofractionation. These findings support SBRT (5 fractions) as
a convenient, effective, and well-tolerated option, pending mature 4-5 year outcomes.
Credit: Dr Rohit Malde MD DNB FRCR (UK)

OligoCare cohort interim analysis: conclusions
SBRT demonstrates durable real-world local control in OMD
(88.6% at 3yrs in more than 2400 pts)
Optimizing minimum PTV dose is essential
(most critical technical factor for success)
De novo OMD shows better local control than repeat OMD,
likely due to a higher delivered dose
Lower local control in CRC (despite receiving the highest
median dose per fraction) highlights its
relative radioresistance and suggests a need for combination
treatment/dose escalation strategies

Take home messages: phase Ill randomized DBCG RT Natural trial
• High quality trial; shows importance of independent data monitoring & planned

• RT + ET gives optimal local control
• RT or ET appear to have similar local control in low-risk group
• Surgery only is associated with high local recurrence rates even in low-risk group

SUMMARY
• POP-RT (2 years ADT) demonstrated significant benefit in bFFS, cFFS, and MFS with WPRT.
• PEACE-2 (3 years ADT, modern imaging, dose-escalated RT) showed no significant improvement in bPFS, MFS, CSS, or OS.
Only a borderline trend in cPFS (HR 0.81, p=0.09). No additional toxicity observed.
Credit: Dr Rohit Malde MD DNB FRCR (UK)
